title: Automated readability gate (multi-formula) + plain-language reviewer workflow
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task implements the automated multi-formula readability gate and the plain-language reviewer workflow so no guide ships above the grade target and a human plain-language reviewer signs off before publication.
objective: Implement a CI readability gate computing reading level with multiple formulas that blocks any guide above the grade target, plus the plain-language reviewer sign-off workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: CI computes reading level (multiple formulas) and blocks any guide above the grade target (<= 8; never above 10)
A plain-language reviewer signs off before publication (recorded in the reviewers ledger)
The gate runs on every guide PR and reports the computed grade level per guide
